ranked_movies.title: Twins
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed as a landmark comedy that defied expectations for Arnold Schwarzenegger, with a famous backend deal that became one of the most profitable in his career. It is highlighted as a perfect mix of hilarious and serious elements.

ranked_movies.title: The Naked Gun
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned across multiple threads as a benchmark for successful comedy, with the new 2025 installment praised for Liam Neeson's performance and the original films celebrated as classics.

ranked_movies.title: Airplane!
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a foundational comedy masterpiece from David Zucker, often referenced in discussions about the greatest comedies and the art of spoof filmmaking.

ranked_movies.title: Kindergarten Cop
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a quintessential Arnold Schwarzenegger comedy that blends action and humor, often listed alongside Twins and True Lies.

ranked_movies.title: True Lies
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as another prime example of Schwarzenegger's successful transition into comedy-action, noted for its rewatchability and perfect tone.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up multiple times in the context of horror and comedy being overlooked by awards, with Toni Collette's performance specifically cited as Oscar-worthy.

ranked_movies.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the debate about genre classification and awards, noted for its comedic elements despite being a multi-genre film.

ranked_movies.title: Little Miss Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced as a rare comedy that received significant Academy Award recognition, with Alan Arkin's win noted.

ranked_movies.title: My Cousin Vinny
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as one of the few full comedies to win a major Oscar (Marisa Tomei for Best Supporting Actress).

ranked_movies.title: Jingle All the Way
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a classic family holiday comedy from the 90s, often mentioned in lists of nostalgic films.

ranked_movies.title: Mrs. Doubtfire
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a top family comedy recommendation from the 90s, celebrated for its heart and humor.

ranked_movies.title: Home Alone
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a quintessential family comedy, a staple of 90s cinema.

ranked_movies.title: The Hangover
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a modern comedy franchise that achieved massive box office success, though later installments were criticized for high budgets.

ranked_movies.title: Superbad
ranked_movies.reason: Implied in discussions about the Apatow-era comedies that dominated the 2000s, representing a peak in studio comedy success.

ranked_movies.title: Dracula
ranked_movies.reason: Francis Ford Coppola's adaptation was heavily debated, with its comedic and erotic tones being a point of discussion compared to the novel.

ranked_movies.title: What We Do in the Shadows
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a masterful comedy series, stemming from the acclaimed film, in a thread asking for comedy suggestions.

ranked_movies.title: Tropic Thunder
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a top-tier comedy movie to watch, noted for its meta-humor and memorable performances.

ranked_movies.title: The Big Short
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as an example of a director (Adam McKay) successfully transitioning from comedy to dramatic filmmaking.

ranked_movies.title: Barry
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a show where a comedic actor (Bill Hader) excelled in a dramatic role, demonstrating the range of comedy performers.

ranked_movies.title: Us
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned alongside Hereditary as a horror film with a phenomenal performance (Lupita Nyong'o) that was overlooked by awards.
